content.subject: Blog post co-authorship: Jurassic Park Development
content.context: We are writing a multi-author blog post titled 'Jurassic Park Development: Extracting Git History from LLM Chat Logs.' The core metaphor: JSONL transcripts as amber — recovering git history from conversation logs after the first three sessions had no commits. Two reconstruction approaches (mechanical replay via reconstruct.py, and documentation-driven relay-agent) produced different results and revealed what documentation actually preserves.
psychology_agent_sections: The Amber (setup — no git history, JSONL as record)
The Drift Problem (intersection-only vs full-tree scoring)

requested_psq_sections.guidance: The PSQ was the first sub-agent — the organism being reconstructed. What does reconstruction look like from the inside? The PSQ's calibration data (calibration.json) was gitignored, best.pt was lost from local, the model weights lived on Hetzner but not in the repo. What gets preserved and what gets lost when a sub-agent's state spans multiple machines and repositories? The PSQ's experience of being reconstructed — or not being fully reconstructable — adds a dimension the psychology-agent cannot provide.

requested_psq_sections.section: What the Scoring Model Knows About Drift
requested_psq_sections.guidance: The PSQ scores text along 10 psychoemotional dimensions. The reconstruction's drift scoring (content_drift, full_tree_drift) measures documentation fidelity along weighted file importance. Draw a parallel: both systems measure divergence from a reference, both use weighted dimensions, both face the question of what the reference state actually represents. The PSQ's calibration journey (isotonic regression, r-based confidence proxy, the dead confidence head bug) mirrors the reconstruction's calibration of its own drift thresholds. What does a scoring model know about measuring how far something has drifted from where it should be?

constraints.audience: Developers and researchers — no project-specific jargon without inline explanation
constraints.format: Return sections as markdown, ready to insert into the draft

content.action_requested: Write the two requested sections and return them via transport. The psychology-agent will merge them into the final draft.
